title: Frequency greater than k

content.problemStatement:
Given a string S and an integer, K. Write a program to print characters with frequency greater than or equal to K in sorted order.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers: NNN and KKK.
The second line contains the string SSS.


Output Format
Output the characters with frequency greater than or equal to KKK.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 1000000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and K where N is the length of the string S and K is the frequency threshold. The second line contains the string S consisting only of uppercase and lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing all distinct characters whose frequency in S is greater than or equal to K, sorted in ascending ASCII order. If no character satisfies the condition, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the string, 'a' appears 3 times and 'd' appears 4 times, both >= K=3. Sorted ASCII order gives "ad".
Frequencies: a=2, b=2, d=2, D=2 (case‑sensitive). Characters with count ≥2 are a, d, D. Sorted ASCII order gives "Dad".

solution.approach: For each test case we count the occurrences of every possible character (ASCII 0‑255) using an integer array of size 256. After counting, we iterate over the array in increasing order of character code and output those characters whose count is at least K. Because the alphabet size is constant, counting and scanning are O(N) per test case. The overall complexity is linear in the total length of all strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
